Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. Deconstruct the Prompt: Input: A pseudo-BPMN representation of a business process. Task: Provide a detailed explanation of the business process. Key Elements to Cover: Purpose of each step. Meaning of the gateways (XOR, AND). How paths converge or loop back. Differences between standard and custom request paths. Conditions triggering approvals or rework steps.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Pseudo-BPMN (Line by Line/Section by Section): Start Event --> Task A: Receive Customer Request Standard start. Purpose: Intake of customer needs. --> Gateway (XOR): Check Request Type XOR (Exclusive Gateway): Only one path can be taken. Purpose: Route based on request classification (Standard vs. Custom). Path 1: [If Type = Standard] Task B1: Perform Standard Validation Purpose: Basic checks for routine requests. --> Gateway (AND): Run Parallel Checks AND (Parallel Gateway): Both paths must execute simultaneously. Task C1: Credit Check & Task C2: Inventory Check Purpose: Assess financial reliability and product availability. --> All Parallel Checks Completed (Join) AND Join: Wait for both C1 and C2 to finish before moving on. --> Task D: Calculate Delivery Date Purpose: Determine when the standard order can be fulfilled. Path 2: [If Type = Custom] Task B2: Perform Custom Feasibility Analysis Purpose: Assess if the special request can actually be done. --> Gateway (XOR): Is Customization Feasible? XOR: Route based on feasibility. [If Yes] Task E1: Prepare Custom Quotation Purpose: Pricing and terms for the custom job. [If No] Task E2: Send Rejection Notice --> End Event Purpose: Inform customer the request cannot be met; process ends for this branch. Convergence & Approval Section: --> After Standard or Custom Path Tasks Completed Implicitly an XOR Join or OR Join (merging Task D and Task E1). Process continues. --> Gateway (XOR): Is Approval Needed? XOR: Route based on business rules (e.g., order value, risk). Path A: [If No Approval Needed] Task G: Generate Final Invoice Straight to invoicing. Path B: [If Yes] Task F: Obtain Manager Approval Purpose: Management oversight. --> Gateway (XOR): Is Approval Granted? XOR: Route based on managers decision. [If Yes] Task G: Generate Final Invoice Approved, move to invoicing. [If No] Task H: Re-evaluate Conditions Rework step. Purpose: Figure out why it was rejected and adjust. --> Loop back to Task E1 (for Custom Path) or Task D (for Standard Path) The loop routing depends on the original request type. If custom, rework the quote. If standard, recalculate the delivery (maybe adjust terms/dates). Final Steps: --> Task I: Send Confirmation to Customer Purpose: Notify customer of successful processing/invoicing. End Event Process concludes.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Structure the Response: Introduction: High-level overview of the process (handling customer requests, branching based on type, approval workflows). Step-by-Step Process Breakdown: Group by logical phases (Intake, Routing, Standard Path, Custom Path, Approval/Rework, Conclusion). Detail the purpose of each task. Gateway Explanations: Explicitly define XOR and AND gateways and explain their specific use in this model. Standard vs. Custom Path Comparison: Contrast the two main branches as requested. Convergence, Approvals, and Loops: Explain how the paths come together, what triggers an approval, and how the rework loop functions. Conclusion: Brief summary.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 4. Drafting the Content (Iterative Refinement): (Self-Correction during drafting): When explaining the loop back from Task H, I need to be very clear that BPMN technically handles this with a merge gateway, but logically, the re-evaluation sends the process backward to different points depending on the initial context (Standard goes to D, Custom goes to E1). (Refining Gateway explanations): Make sure to distinguish between the Fork (splitting) and Join (converging) aspects of the AND gateway, as this is a crucial BPMN concept. (Clarity on Approvals): The prompt asks under what conditions approvals are triggered. Since the pseudo-BPMN doesnt specify the exact business rule (e.g., >$10k), I should state that it depends on configurable business rules (like order value, risk, or specific custom requirements), rather than making up a specific dollar amount.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. Final Review against Prompt Requirements: Detailed explanation of the business process? Yes. Purpose of each step? Yes. Meaning of gateways? Yes. How paths converge or loop back? Yes. How tasks differ based on standard vs. custom? Yes. Conditions for approvals or rework? Yes.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 6. Final Polish: Ensure tone is professional, analytical, and easy to read using formatting (bullet points, bold text). (This leads to the final provided output).
